destinations.evidence: Port-generated scheduled system transactions set TxScheduled.to to get_scheduler_address() and calldata to Scheduler.execute(callId,index), while call.value/input metadata comes from the stored call.
destinations.snippet:
let input = executeCall { callId: U256::from(obligation.call_id), executionIndex: U256::from(obligation.index) }.abi_encode().into();
let tx = TxScheduled::new_with_options(..., Some(get_scheduler_address()), call.value, input, origin_hash, obligation.call_id, obligation.index, ...);

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port: scheduled system transactions target Scheduler, not the stored call target.
rationale: The current transaction construction path obtains the scheduler address from SynchronousScheduler and passes it as TxScheduled.to; the stored call.to is executed by the Scheduler contract, not used as the outer transaction recipient.
statement: A scheduled system transaction created for a stored call must set its transaction recipient to the configured Scheduler contract address so it invokes Scheduler.execute(callId, executionIndex), not to the stored call.to target address.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a stored call with call.to != Scheduler and assert synthesized TxScheduled.to == Scheduler and calldata decodes to execute(callId,index).
summary: Scheduled system transactions target the Scheduler contract.
title: Scheduled system transactions target the Scheduler contract
